slides.title: The warning that shows up *years early*
slides.sub: Blood sugar is often the last metric to break. Fasting insulin can speak up long before it does.

slides.title: Insulin moves first
slides.body: Years before sugar rises, your body quietly makes *more and more insulin* to keep glucose normal. The number looks fine while the strain builds.

slides.title: Fasting insulin
slides.body: A simple fasting blood test. A low fasting insulin suggests your cells still respond easily. A creeping one *can hint at* early resistance.

slides.title: HOMA-IR, one ratio
slides.body: HOMA-IR pairs fasting insulin with fasting glucose into a single score. It is one of the *earliest readouts* of how your metabolism is coping.

slides.title: Metabolic aging, decoded
slides.body: Insulin resistance is linked to faster aging across the heart, brain and waistline. Catching it early means it is *often still reversible*.

slides.title: The levers you control
slides.body: Strength work, walks after meals, deep sleep and fewer refined carbs can support insulin sensitivity. Daily habits *move this number* more than willpower.

slides.title: Track the habits that *move the needle*
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the routines that support insulin sensitivity are easy to see and repeat.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your blood sugar can read perfectly while insulin resistance has been building for years. Fasting insulin and HOMA-IR are the early warning most people never check. 🌿

Insulin rises first, the HOMA-IR ratio catches it early, and the right daily habits can still turn it around. Save this for your next blood panel. 🔖
